title: Italian Free-Guarantee Ruling Boosts Transfer Pricing Toolkit
summary: Italian Free-Guarantee Ruling Boosts Transfer Pricing Toolkit news.bloombergtax.com
ai_summary: An Italian court ruling concerning "free-guarantees" is set to enhance the transfer pricing toolkit for financial transactions. This decision provides critical guidance on the arm's length valuation and implications of intra-group financial guarantees within the Italian tax landscape.

title: Italian Free-Guarantee Ruling Boosts Transfer Pricing Toolkit
summary: Italian Free-Guarantee Ruling Boosts Transfer Pricing Toolkit news.bloombergtax.com
ai_summary: An Italian court ruling concerning "free-guarantees" is set to enhance the transfer pricing toolkit for financial transactions. This decision provides critical guidance on the arm's length valuation and implications of intra-group financial guarantees within the Italian tax landscape.

title: Rajasthan HC Quashes Penalty against ITO As Error of Judgment Is Not Misconduct
summary: The Court held that an Assessing Officer’s quasi-judicial decision cannot attract disciplinary action merely because another view was possible. Absence of mala fides or lack of integrity defeated the misconduct charge. The post Rajasthan HC Quashes Penalty against ITO As Error of Judgment Is Not Misconduct appeared first on TaxGuru .
ai_summary: The Rajasthan High Court quashed a penalty against an Assessing Officer, ruling that an error of judgment does not constitute misconduct for disciplinary action. The court emphasized that a quasi-judicial decision cannot solely lead to penalties if there's no mala fide intent or lack of integrity.

title: ITAT Remands TP Adjustment Issue as RPT Filter Was Not Properly Verified
summary: The Hyderabad ITAT found contradictions in the TPO’s reasoning for excluding a comparable company due to alleged lack of RPT data. The Tribunal directed fresh verification and recomputation of the RPT filter before deciding whether the company should remain excluded. The post ITAT Remands TP Adjustment Issue as RPT Filter Was Not Properly Verified appeared first on TaxGuru .
ai_summary: The Hyderabad ITAT remanded a transfer pricing adjustment, citing contradictions in the Transfer Pricing Officer's rationale for excluding a comparable based on related party transaction (RPT) data. The Tribunal mandated a fresh verification and recomputation of the RPT filter for comparable selection.

title: Section 68 Dispute Remanded as Source of Unlisted Share Funds Remained Unverified
summary: The Tribunal held that although the assessee produced documentary evidence supporting the loan, questions regarding the lender’s financial capacity and source of funds required deeper examination. The matter was remanded to the Assessing Officer for fresh verification. The post Section 68 Dispute Remanded as Source of Unlisted Share Funds Remained Unverified appeared first on TaxGuru .
ai_summary: The Tribunal remanded a Section 68 dispute regarding unexplained funds for unlisted shares, requiring deeper verification of the lender's financial capacity and source of funds. Despite documentary evidence, the Assessing Officer was directed to re-examine the matter.

title: ITAT Allows Appeal as DVO Reference Under Section 55A Was Invalid for AY 2010-11
summary: The Tribunal held that the Assessing Officer could not refer the property valuation to the DVO under the unamended Section 55A. Since the reference itself was invalid, the addition based on the DVOs valuation could not survive. The post ITAT Allows Appeal as DVO Reference Under Section 55A Was Invalid for AY 2010-11 appeared first on TaxGuru .
ai_summary: The Tribunal ruled that the Assessing Officer's reference for property valuation to the DVO under the unamended Section 55A for AY 2010-11 was invalid. Consequently, the addition based on the DVO's valuation was set aside.

title: ITAT Deletes LTCG Addition as DVO Reference Under Section 55A Was Not Valid
summary: ITAT Surat held that the Assessing Officer could not refer the property valuation to the DVO where the assessee had adopted a higher value based on a registered valuer’s report. The LTCG addition was deleted. The post ITAT Deletes LTCG Addition as DVO Reference Under Section 55A Was Not Valid appeared first on TaxGuru .
ai_summary: ITAT Surat deleted a long-term capital gains (LTCG) addition, holding that the Assessing Officer's reference to the DVO for property valuation was invalid. This was applicable where the assessee had already adopted a higher value based on a registered valuer's report.

title: Capital Gains Relief Granted as Section 55A Amendment Was Prospective: ITAT Surat
summary: The Tribunal held that the Assessing Officer could not validly refer the valuation issue to the DVO for Assessment Year 2012-13. Since the statutory conditions were not met, the long-term capital gains addition was deleted. The post Capital Gains Relief Granted as Section 55A Amendment Was Prospective: ITAT Surat appeared first on TaxGuru .
ai_summary: ITAT Surat granted capital gains relief, asserting that the amendment to Section 55A was prospective and thus not applicable for AY 2012-13. The Tribunal concluded that the Assessing Officer's valuation reference to the DVO was invalid as statutory conditions were not met.

title: ITAT Deletes LTCG Addition as DVO Reference Under Section 55A Was Invalid
summary: ITAT Surat held that the Assessing Officer could not refer the property valuation to the DVO where the assessee had adopted a higher value based on a registered valuer’s report. The LTCG addition based on the DVO report was deleted. The post ITAT Deletes LTCG Addition as DVO Reference Under Section 55A Was Invalid appeared first on TaxGuru .
ai_summary: ITAT Surat deleted a long-term capital gains (LTCG) addition, determining that the Assessing Officer's referral for property valuation to the DVO under Section 55A was invalid. The assessee's higher value, based on a registered valuer’s report, was upheld over the DVO's valuation.

title: ITAT Deletes LTCG Addition as DVO Reference Was Invalid for Pre-July 2012 Transaction
summary: The Tribunal held that a land sale completed before 01.07.2012 could not be subjected to a DVO reference under the amended Section 55A. Since the valuation reference was invalid, the long-term capital gains addition was deleted. The post ITAT Deletes LTCG Addition as DVO Reference Was Invalid for Pre-July 2012 Transaction appeared first on TaxGuru .
ai_summary: The Tribunal deleted a long-term capital gains (LTCG) addition, ruling that a land sale completed before July 1, 2012, could not be subjected to a DVO reference under the amended Section 55A. Consequently, the valuation reference was deemed invalid, and the addition was deleted.

title: ITAT Allows Appeal as DVO Reference Was Invalid Under Pre-2012 Section 55A
summary: The Tribunal held that the Assessing Officer could not validly refer the property valuation to the DVO for Assessment Year 2012-13 under the unamended Section 55A. Since the valuation reference lacked legal authority, the addition to long-term capital gains could not be sustained. The post ITAT Allows Appeal as DVO Reference Was Invalid Under Pre-2012 Section 55A appeared first on TaxGuru .
ai_summary: The Tribunal allowed an appeal, concluding that the Assessing Officer's property valuation reference to the DVO for AY 2012-13 was invalid under the unamended Section 55A. The addition to long-term capital gains, based on this unauthorized reference, could not be sustained.

title: Assessee Gets Relief as Section 55A Amendment Was Prospective, Not Retrospective
summary: The Tribunal held that the amended Section 55A could not be applied to a land sale completed before 01.07.2012. Since the DVO reference was not valid under the law then in force, the capital gains addition based on that valuation could not stand. The post Assessee Gets Relief as Section 55A Amendment Was Prospective, Not Retrospective appeared first on TaxGuru .
ai_summary: The Tribunal granted relief to an assessee, ruling that the amended Section 55A could not be applied retrospectively to a land sale completed before July 1, 2012. The capital gains addition based on the invalid DVO valuation was therefore set aside.

title: Registered Valuer’s Report Prevails as DVO Reference Failed Legal Requirements
summary: The Tribunal held that the amendment to Section 55A applies prospectively and could not be used for land transactions completed before 01.07.2012. As the assessee had relied on a registered valuers higher valuation, the DVO reference was declared invalid and the appeal was allowed. The post Registered Valuer’s Report Prevails as DVO Reference Failed Legal Requirements appeared first on TaxGuru .
ai_summary: The Tribunal held that the amendment to Section 55A applies prospectively, invalidating a DVO reference for land transactions prior to July 1, 2012. The assessee's reliance on a registered valuer's higher valuation was upheld, and the appeal was allowed.

title: ITAT Allows Registered Valuer’s Valuation as Section 55A DVO Reference Was Invalid
summary: The Tribunal held that the Assessing Officer could not validly refer the matter to the DVO for a transaction completed before the 2012 amendment to Section 55A. Capital gains were directed to be recomputed using the registered valuers report. The post ITAT Allows Registered Valuer’s Valuation as Section 55A DVO Reference Was Invalid appeared first on TaxGuru .
ai_summary: The Tribunal permitted the use of a registered valuer's valuation, finding the Assessing Officer's DVO reference invalid for transactions completed before the 2012 amendment to Section 55A. Capital gains were consequently directed to be recomputed based on the registered valuer's report.

title: ITAT Deletes Capital Gains Addition as Section 55A DVO Reference Was Invalid
summary: The Tribunal held that for AY 2011-12, the Assessing Officer could not refer property valuation to the DVO when the assessee relied on a registered valuers higher FMV. The capital gains computation based on the DVOs valuation was therefore set aside. The post ITAT Deletes Capital Gains Addition as Section 55A DVO Reference Was Invalid appeared first on TaxGuru .
ai_summary: The Tribunal deleted a capital gains addition for AY 2011-12, determining that the Assessing Officer could not refer property valuation to the DVO when the assessee relied on a registered valuer's higher fair market value. The capital gains computation based on the DVO's valuation was set aside.

title: Double Taxation Claim not Allowed as Income Actually Belonged to Assessee
summary: The Bombay High Court held that an assessee cannot avoid tax merely because the same amount was taxed in another entity’s hands when the income actually belonged to the assessee. The Court upheld the addition of ₹1.71 crore arising from under-invoiced export transactions. The post Double Taxation Claim not Allowed as Income Actually Belonged to Assessee appeared first on TaxGuru .
ai_summary: The Bombay High Court denied a double taxation claim, upholding an income addition of ₹1.71 crore arising from under-invoiced export transactions. The court affirmed that income belongs to the assessee who actually earned it, a principle highly relevant to transfer pricing and profit attribution in cross-border dealings.

title: DVO Reference Held Invalid as Assessee’s Valuation Exceeded FMV: Bombay HC
summary: The Bombay High Court upheld the Tribunal’s view that a reference under Section 55A was invalid where the assessee’s valuation was higher than the fair market value. The appeal was dismissed as no question of law arose. The post DVO Reference Held Invalid as Assessee’s Valuation Exceeded FMV: Bombay HC appeared first on TaxGuru .
ai_summary: The Bombay High Court upheld a Tribunal's decision that a reference under Section 55A to a Departmental Valuation Officer (DVO) was invalid when the assessee’s valuation of a capital asset exceeded its fair market value. This ruling clarifies procedural aspects of property valuation for general income tax purposes in India.

title: AO Cannot Refer Property Valuation to DVO When Assessee’s Value Exceeds FMV: Bombay HC
summary: The Bombay High Court held that a DVO reference under Section 55A was invalid because the assessee’s declared value was higher than the fair market value. The ruling reiterates the scope of Section 55A as it existed before the 2012 amendment. The post AO Cannot Refer Property Valuation to DVO When Assessee’s Value Exceeds FMV: Bombay HC appeared first on TaxGuru .
ai_summary: The Bombay High Court ruled that an Assessing Officer cannot refer property valuation to a Departmental Valuation Officer (DVO) under Section 55A if the assessee’s declared value already exceeds the fair market value. This decision clarifies the scope of Section 55A as it existed before the 2012 amendment for general tax valuation in India.

title: No Double Taxation on Liability Write-Back: Delhi ITAT Deletes Section 41(1) Addition and Notional Interest Demand
summary: The Delhi ITAT held that liabilities already written back and offered to tax in later years cannot be taxed again under Section 41(1). The Tribunal ruled that such an addition would result in impermissible double taxation. The post No Double Taxation on Liability Write-Back: Delhi ITAT Deletes Section 41(1) Addition and Notional Interest Demand appeared first on TaxGuru .
ai_summary: The Delhi ITAT ruled against double taxation on liability write-backs, holding that liabilities already written back and offered to tax in later years cannot be taxed again under Section 41(1). The Tribunal deleted additions made under Section 41(1) and quashed notional interest demands, focusing on general income tax principles regarding cessation of liabilities.

title: Changed Jobs Mid-Year? How to File ITR with Two Form 16s
summary: Employees changing jobs may face additional tax at filing because each employer deducts TDS independently. The demand usually reflects a TDS shortfall, not double taxation. The post Changed Jobs Mid-Year? How to File ITR with Two Form 16s appeared first on TaxGuru .
ai_summary: This article provides guidance for employees in India who have changed jobs mid-year, explaining how to file their Income Tax Return (ITR) with two Form 16s. It clarifies that any additional tax demand typically arises from a Tax Deducted at Source (TDS) shortfall due to independent employer deductions, rather than actual double taxation for individuals.

title: For TP comparability, functional similarity and scale of operations were crucial; inclusion of low-turnover comparable was set aside
summary: Where ITAT directed inclusion of Cepha Imaging Pvt. Ltd. as a comparable solely on the basis that it satisfied the export turnover filter without examining its functional comparability with assessee, and further directed inclusion of CG Vak Software & Exports Ltd. The post For TP comparability, functional similarity and scale of operations were crucial; inclusion of low-turnover comparable was set aside appeared first on TaxGuru .
ai_summary: The Income Tax Appellate Tribunal (ITAT) emphasized the critical importance of functional similarity and scale of operations for transfer pricing comparability analysis in India. The tribunal set aside the inclusion of a low-turnover comparable, such as Cepha Imaging Pvt. Ltd., that was based solely on an export turnover filter without adequate functional examination.

title: Pune ITAT Deletes ₹5.67 Crore TP Adjustment; TPO Cannot Cherry-Pick One Transaction While Accepting TNMM for Others
summary: Pune ITAT held that once TNMM is accepted for a taxpayer’s aggregated international transactions, the TPO cannot isolate a single transaction and apply a different method. The ruling deleted the transfer pricing adjustment and reinforced consistency in benchmarking. The post Pune ITAT Deletes ₹5.67 Crore TP Adjustment; TPO Cannot Cherry-Pick One Transaction While Accepting TNMM for Others appeared first on TaxGuru .
ai_summary: The Pune ITAT deleted a ₹5.67 Crore transfer pricing adjustment, ruling that the Tax Officer (TPO) cannot cherry-pick a single transaction for a different method after accepting TNMM for the taxpayer’s aggregated international transactions. This decision reinforces the principle of consistent benchmarking methodology in India.

title: Entire Sale Consideration Cannot Be Taxed as Capital Gain: Mumbai ITAT Orders Fresh Computation
summary: Mumbai ITAT ruled that where a capital asset was acquired before 01.04.2001, the claim for adopting fair market value as on that date must be examined on merits. The key takeaway is that statutory valuation rights cannot be rejected on technical grounds alone. The post Entire Sale Consideration Cannot Be Taxed as Capital Gain: Mumbai ITAT Orders Fresh Computation appeared first on TaxGuru .
ai_summary: The Mumbai ITAT ruled that the fair market value of capital assets acquired before April 1, 2001, must be considered for fresh capital gains computation. This decision ensures that statutory valuation rights are examined on merits, preventing rejection on technical grounds alone.

title: Section 80IB Deduction Cannot Be Reduced by Section 80HHC Deduction During Computation: ITAT Delhi
summary: The dispute concerned whether deduction under Section 80IB should be reduced by the amount already allowed under Section 80HHC. ITAT held that deductions can be computed independently, provided the aggregate deduction does not exceed eligible profits. The post Section 80IB Deduction Cannot Be Reduced by Section 80HHC Deduction During Computation: ITAT Delhi appeared first on TaxGuru .
ai_summary: The ITAT Delhi ruled that deductions under Section 80IB and Section 80HHC should be computed independently. The tribunal clarified that the aggregate deduction should not exceed eligible profits, preventing reduction of 80IB by amounts allowed under 80HHC.

title: ALP Cannot Be Determined at Nil Without Comparable Analysis: ITAT Ahmedabad

ai_summary: The ITAT Ahmedabad ruled that the arm's length price for intra-group services cannot be determined at nil without proper comparable analysis. This decision challenges the TPO's ability to arbitrarily disallow a price based on assumptions of benefit or commercial necessity.

title: ITAT Orders De Novo Adjudication on Taxability of Concessional Sugar Distributed by Co-operative Sugar Factory
summary: Tribunal held that the differential amount between the levy price of sugar and the concessional price at which it was sold by a co-operative factory to its members and non-members constitutes an unallowable appropriation of profit to be added to business income, or a valid business practice could not be decided without examining the factual parameters mandated by the Supreme Court in Krishna Sahakari Karkhana Ltd. The appeal was allowed by applying the enhanced exemption limit. The post Leave Encashment Addition Deleted as CBDT Increased Exemption Ceiling to ₹25 Lakh appeared first on TaxGuru .
ai_summary: The ITAT ruled to delete a leave encashment addition, citing the CBDT's increased exemption ceiling of ₹25 lakh. The decision allowed the full exemption of the amount received on retirement by applying the revised limit.

title: ITAT Deletes TDS Disallowance as Corresponding Income Was Settled Under VSVS
summary: The ITAT Bangalore held that disallowance under Section 40(a)(i) could not survive once the recipient of the income settled the tax dispute under the Vivad se Vishwas Scheme, 2024. The ruling relied on CBDT Circular No.19/2024 and FAQ No.58 granting consequential relief to the deductor. The post ITAT Deletes TDS Disallowance as Corresponding Income Was Settled Under VSVS appeared first on TaxGuru .
ai_summary: The ITAT Bangalore ruled that a TDS disallowance under Section 40(a)(i) cannot be sustained if the corresponding income was settled under the Vivad se Vishwas Scheme, 2024. The decision, relying on CBDT guidance, provides consequential relief to the deductor.

title: Only Net Profit Taxable on Unaccounted Sales, No Double Additions: Delhi ITAT
summary: The ITAT held that once profit is estimated on unaccounted sales, separate additions for wages and operational expenditure cannot be made again under section 69C. The ruling treated such additions as double taxation of the same income stream. The post Only Net Profit Taxable on Unaccounted Sales, No Double Additions: Delhi ITAT appeared first on TaxGuru .
ai_summary: The Delhi ITAT ruled that only net profit should be taxable on unaccounted sales, preventing double additions for wages and operational expenditure under Section 69C. The decision clarifies principles against taxing the same income stream twice.

title: No Section 68 Addition on Demonetisation Cash Deposits Supported by Business Receipts: ITAT Delhi
summary: The ITAT held that cash deposits during demonetisation could not be treated as unexplained when the assessee had disclosed sales and provided debtor details. The Tribunal ruled that taxing the same receipts again under Section 68 would amount to double taxation. The post No Section 68 Addition on Demonetisation Cash Deposits Supported by Business Receipts: ITAT Delhi appeared first on TaxGuru .
ai_summary: The ITAT Delhi ruled against Section 68 additions for cash deposits made during demonetisation, provided the assessee supported them with disclosed sales and debtor details. The Tribunal emphasized that taxing such legitimate business receipts again would constitute double taxation.

title: ITAT Excludes Manufacturing Companies as Comparables as Assessee Was Predominantly a Trader
summary: The Bangalore ITAT upheld exclusion of manufacturing entities from transfer pricing comparables after finding that the assessee’s activities were mainly trading in nature. The Tribunal held that manufacturers and traders differ significantly in functions, assets, and risks. The post ITAT Excludes Manufacturing Companies as Comparables as Assessee Was Predominantly a Trader appeared first on TaxGuru .
ai_summary: The Bangalore ITAT upheld the exclusion of manufacturing companies as comparables in a transfer pricing case, affirming that the assessee was predominantly a trader. The tribunal's decision underscored the critical importance of functional comparability, noting significant differences in functions, assets, and risks between manufacturers and traders.

title: Rework Transfer Pricing Addition as Cyber Media Research Was Comparable to Support Services: ITAT Mumbai
summary: Tribunal directed inclusion of Cyber Media Research Limited after finding that market research and consultancy services were comparable to the assessee’s support service activities. Earlier Tribunal rulings supporting comparability were followed. The post Rework Transfer Pricing Addition as Cyber Media Research Was Comparable to Support Services: ITAT Mumbai appeared first on TaxGuru .
ai_summary: The ITAT Mumbai directed the re-computation of a transfer pricing addition by including Cyber Media Research Limited as a comparable. The tribunal found that the company's market research and consultancy services were comparable to the assessee’s support service activities, aligning with previous rulings on comparability.

summary: Euromoney’s 2021 Transfer Pricing Advisers Guide recognises 150 Deloitte leaders Deloitte

title: CJEU clarifies that intragroup transfer pricing adjustments require at least a contractual basis to be treated as a supply of services - Deloitte
summary: CJEU clarifies that intragroup transfer pricing adjustments require at least a contractual basis to be treated as a supply of services Deloitte
ai_summary: The CJEU clarified that intragroup transfer pricing adjustments require at least a contractual basis to be recognized as a supply of services. This ruling from the European Union's highest court provides critical guidance on the legal underpinning required for intercompany transactions involving transfer pricing.
